title: Find the cause — stale rows after back navigation
question: Why does back navigation intermittently show rows from the previous filter?
lede: Reproduced three times in QA: filter by ?tag=review, click through to ?tag=git, press back — sometimes the git rows linger under the review URL. Four falsifiable hypotheses, each with its probe and observation; judge them and record the diagnosis.
inputs: qa notes 2026-07-23 — repro steps and frequency
browser devtools traces from the repro session
conclusionNote: The diagnosis must name the validated cause and why the falsified hypotheses are dead — a fix is a separate, later decision.
conclusionPlaceholder: e.g. H2 is the cause: bfcache restores the pre-navigation DOM and popstate does not fire on restore; H1/H3 falsified by direct probes.

causalMap.caption: Each hypothesis attacks one edge of the render path: H1 the event, H2 the cache restore, H3 the filter, H4 the timing between them.

items.label: popstate never fires on back
items.predicts: A log line inside the popstate handler would never appear on any back navigation.
items.probe: Instrumented the handler and pressed back ten times across Safari and Chrome.
items.observed: The handler fired on every non-reproducing traversal and on all Chrome traversals — the event wiring itself is sound.

items.label: bfcache restores the old DOM without any event
items.predicts: Reproducing traversals would show a pageshow event with persisted=true and no popstate at all.
items.probe: Logged pageshow.persisted alongside popstate during twenty Safari traversals.
items.observed: All three reproductions showed persisted=true with no popstate; every clean traversal showed a normal load or a popstate. Exact correlation.

items.label: filterByTag returns stale or reordered results
items.predicts: Calling the pure function directly with the review tag would sometimes return git rows.
items.probe: Property-tested filterByTag with the full dataset and both tags, five thousand iterations in Node.
items.observed: Identical, order-stable output every iteration — the pure core cannot produce the symptom.

items.label: A render race lets the old list win
items.predicts: Two renderFromUrl invocations would interleave, with the earlier one finishing last.
items.probe: Added invocation counters; renderFromUrl is synchronous end to end — there is nothing to interleave.
items.observed: Single invocation per event in every trace; the function has no await points.

unresolved: whether the eventual fix listens to pageshow or opts out of bfcache — a change-blueprint question, not a diagnosis one
